string-array.name: perf_overlay_display_items_names
string-array.item: 🎬 Resolution & Target FPS
Codec Decoder Info
Rx/Rd Received & Rendered FPS
📶 Packet Loss
🌐 Bitrate & Network Latency
⏱️/🥵 Decode Latency (normal < 15ms)
🖥 Host Latency
🔋 Battery Level
📉 1% Low FPS (Smoothness)

string-array.name: perf_overlay_display_items_values
string-array.item: resolution
decoder
render_fps
packet_loss
network_latency
decode_latency
host_latency
battery
one_percent_low

string-array.name: tip_messages
string-array.item: 💡 Tip: It\'s recommended to use 5GHz WiFi for a better experience, lightning fast speeds~
💡 Tip: The wallpaper API option can set the API for random wallpaper sources, or a single image URL
💡 Tip: Click the floating menu button in the **top-right corner** of the screen to open a brand new control panel with adaptive landscape/portrait layout, all settings at your fingertips~
💡 Tip: The control panel supports multi-level back navigation. Press the back key to close step by step, from sub-settings to the panel to hiding, clear steps without getting lost~
💡 Tip: Enable auto-hide for the control panel in "More" settings. The panel will automatically retract after 2 seconds of inactivity, keeping your game screen clean and unobstructed~
💡 Tip: Using "Balanced" frame rate mode provides the best balance between frame rate and latency, perfect!

💡 Tip: Enabling HDR provides better color and contrast performance. Binding the corresponding HDR profile in the Sunshine base version can more accurately sync with your client screen, vibrant colors~
💡 Tip: Adjusting resolution can balance quality and performance, find the settings that work best for you~
💡 Tip: The performance overlay can be displayed in any of the four corners, or at the top/bottom edge
💡 Tip: The items displayed in the performance overlay are customizable. Select the metrics you want to see in settings, tailor your monitoring panel to your needs~
💡 Tip: When using HEVC or AV1 decoders, you can adjust the bitrate during streaming without exiting, finding the optimal bitrate for your network and device~

💡 Tip: The control panel supports three touch modes — Enhanced Multi-Touch for mobile games, Trackpad Mode for mouse simulation ideal for office work, and Classic Mouse Mode with local pointer for better responsiveness~
💡 Tip: In Trackpad Mode, you can adjust sensitivity (1-200 levels), enable double-tap drag and local cursor rendering for silky smooth mouse control~
💡 Tip: After enabling key mapping, you can overlay virtual controller buttons on the streaming screen, supporting multiple profiles and quick switching between different games~
💡 Tip: The key mapping editor supports grid snapping. When enabled, dragging buttons automatically aligns them to grid lines, making it easy to create a clean button layout~
💡 Tip: You can adjust the button layer order in the editor, moving up or down to change the display hierarchy of overlapping buttons, ensuring every button can be correctly touched~
💡 Tip: You can create combo key buttons to send key combinations like Ctrl+Shift+Esc with a single tap, making operations more efficient~
💡 Tip: Key mapping supports custom colors for each button (normal state, pressed state, background color, text color), letting virtual buttons perfectly blend into the game screen~
💡 Tip: Quick action cards support long-press drag to reorder. Put your most used functions at the front for one-tap access anytime~
💡 Tip: After enabling clipboard sync, text copied on your phone is automatically synced to the host, and vice versa, making cross-device pasting super convenient~
💡 Tip: The "Auto Optimize" option in host settings can optimize host performance when starting streaming, making games run smoother~
💡 Tip: Enable the pause streaming feature in "More" settings. When you don\'t need the stream temporarily, you can pause transmission to save bandwidth and battery~
💡 Tip: You can toggle the microphone directly in the control panel without exiting the stream and going into system settings, convenient and fast~
💡 Tip: The transparency of the floating button can be adjusted in "More" settings (10%-100%) to suit different background screens~

💡 Tip: When network quality severely drops, the red banner auto-dismisses after 10 seconds, and the orange one after 5 seconds. It also clears automatically when network recovers, without interrupting your game~
💡 Tip: You can export key mapping configurations for use on other devices. It will automatically adapt based on screen parameters, so one setup works for both tablets and phones~
